जब琈क, म瀇य 灹देश रा榖य म犆 अवि瀡थत रीवा से म瀇य 灹देश/उ瀄तर 灹देश सीमा तक रा瀠灩ीय राजमाग榁 सं. 7 के िवकास के िलए 30 िसत瀓बर, 2009 को के瀈灶ीय सरकार और म瀇य 灹देश रा榖य सरकार के बीच एक समझौता 榀ापन पर ह瀡ता楹र 琈कए गए थे । और जब琈क, म瀇य 灹देश रा榖य सरकार क琉 ओर से काया榁瀈वयन 灹ािधकरण के 牲प म犆 काय榁 करने के िलए म瀇य 灹देश रा榖य सरकार ने आदेश सं.5593/6502/19/यो./2011 琈दनांक 18.10.2011 के तहत म瀇य 灹देश सड़क िवकास िनगम िल., भोपाल (जो इसके बाद ‘‘灹ािधकरण’’ के 牲प म犆 उि瀖लिखत है) को अिधकृत 琈कया था । और जब琈क, 灹ािधकरण ने म瀇य 灹देश रा榖य म犆 िडजाइन, िनमा榁ण, िव瀄तपोषण, 灹चालन और ह瀡तांतरण (डीबीएफओटी) आधार पर रा瀠灩ीय राजमाग榁 सं. 7 के 琈कमी 229/100 से 琈कमी 140/600 [िडजाइन लंबाई 琈कमी 89.23] तक रा瀠灩ीय राजमाग榁 सं.7 के रीवा से म瀇य 灹देश/उ瀄तर 灹देश सीमा खंड (जो इसके आगे ‘‘किथत खंड’’ के 牲प म犆 उि瀖लिखत है) के िवकास एवं 灹चालन के िलए 琈दनांक 02.09.2011 को 灹瀡तुत िनिवदा के िलए मै. िव瀈瀇याचल ए榉स灹ैसवे 灹ा.िल. िजसका पंजीकृत काया榁लय ए-104, शपथ-4, कणा榁वती 榉लब के सामने, एस.जी. रोड, अहमदाबाद, िपन-380051, है (जो इसके आगे ‘‘琇रयायत灡ाही’’ के 牲प म犆 उि瀖लिखत है) के साथ 25.01.2012 को एक करार (जो इसके आगे ‘‘करार’’ के 牲प म犆 उि瀖लिखत है), 琈कया है । अत: अब, बोली 灹ाि瀉त क琉 तारीख तक यथा-संशोिधत रा瀠灩ीय राजमाग榁 शु瀖क (दर犘 का िनधा榁रण और सं灡हण) िनयम, 2008 के िनयम 3 के साथ प琇ठत और पथकर 瀉लाजा से 20 琈कमी क琉 दूरी के भीतर िनवास करने वाले 瀘यि榉त के िलए मािसक पास क琉 दर वष榁 2014-15 के िलए 225.00 牱. (दो सौ प榔चीस 牱पए मा灴) है और यह राष्灩ीय राजमाग榁 फ琉स (दर犘 का िनधा榁रण और सं灡हण) िनयम, 2008 के उपबंध犘 के अनुसार 灹瀄येक वष榁 संशोधन के अ瀇यधीन है । 4. राजमाग榁 खंड पर ब牣ल या灴ा猒 के िलए िन瀓निलिखत दर犘 पर पास जारी हेतु िनधा榁琇रत दर क琉 50% होगी, बशत犇 琈क ऐसे वािणि榖यक वाहन犘 के उपयोग के िलए कोई स琔वस रोड या वैकि瀖पक सड़क उपल瀑ध न ह犘 । 6. 琈कसी 炈ेणी िवशेष के िलए िविन琖द瀠ट अनुम瀄य भार से अिधक भार वाले यांि灴क वाहन के चालक वाहन से अितभार को उतारे िबना वह उस राजमाग榁 खंड का इ瀡तेमाल करने का हकदार नह牷 होगा । 7. सभी प琇रभाषाएं, यांि灴क वाहन犘 क琉 炈ेणी, इनके अनुम瀄य भार, छूट 灹ा瀉त वाहन犘 क琉 炈ेणी, स琔वस रोड, वैकि瀖पक सड़क इ瀄या琈द किथत वष榁 2007-08 के िलए 灹ित 琈कमी फ琉स क琉 आधार दर के आधार पर यांि灴क वाहन से 灹भा琇रत क琉 जाने वाली वा瀡तिवक रािश और छूट का आकलन 灹ािधकरण 獧ारा राजमाग榁 प琇रयोजना क琉 लंबाई के पूरे 牣ए भाग के आधार पर 琈कया जाएगा । सिचव -- 3 of 6 -- 4 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 30th December, 2014 S.O. 3317(E).—Whereas, by notification of the Government of India in the erstwhile Ministry of Road Transport and Highways number S.O. 275 (E) dated 14th February, 2012 issued under Section 5 of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), the Central Government has entrusted National Highway No. 7 in the State of Madhya Pradesh to the State Government of Madhya Pradesh. And whereas, a Memorandum of Understanding was entered between Central Government and the State Government of Madhya Pradesh on September 30, 2009 for the development of National Highway No. 7 from Rewa to MP/UP Border situated in the State of Madhya Pradesh. And whereas, the State Government of Madhya Pradesh authorized Madhya Pradesh Road Development Corporation Limited, Bhopal (hereinafter referred to as the “Authority”) to act as implementing authority on behalf of the State Government of Madhya Pradesh vide order No. 5593/6502/19/ ;ks./2011 dated 18.10.2011. And whereas, the Authority has entered into an Agreement on 25.01.2012 (hereinafter referred to as said “Agreement”) with M/s. Vindhyachal Expressway Private Limited, having its Registered Office at A-104, Shapath-4, Opp. Karnavati Club, S.G. Road, Ahmedabad 380 051, Gujarat (hereinafter referred to as “Concessionaire”), for the bid submitted on 02.09.2011 for the development & operation of Rewa-MP/UP Border Section of NH-7 from km 229/100 to km 140/600 [Design length 89.23 km] (hereinafter referred to as the “said section”) in the State of Madhya Pradesh on Design, Build, Finance, Operate and Transfer (DBFOT) basis; Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by Section 8A of the National Highways Act, 1956 (48 of 1956), read with Rule 3 of the National Highways Fee (Determination of Rates and Collection) Rules, 2008 and amended upto the date of receipt of bid, (herein referred to as the “Rules”), the Central Government, having regard to the expenditure involved in building, maintenance, management and operation of the said section, interest on the capital invested, reasonable return, the volume of traffic and the period of the Agreement entered into between the Authority and the Concessionaire, hereby notifies that there shall be levied and collected fees on mechanical vehicles for the use of the said section at the rate specified in column (2) of the Table 1 for net road section of 61.33 km length for use of the four and more lane sections (hereinafter referred to as “base rate”) and at one and half times of the base rate for six number of bypasses having total design length of 27.90 km on the Types of Vehicles specified in column (1) of the Table 1 below, and authorizes the said Concessionaire to collect and retain the fees on and from the date of commercial operation dated (COD) declared by the Authority and on getting the completion certificate or the provisional completion certificate as the case may be from the Authority or publication of this notification in the Official Gazette, whichever is later, till the termination date as specified in the Agreement; TABLE 1 Type of Vehicle Base rate of fee per km for the base year 2007-08 (In Rupees) (1) (2) Car, Jeep, Van or Light Motor Vehicle. 0.65 Light Commercial Vehicle, Light Goods Vehicle or Mini Bus. 1.05 Bus or Truck (Two Axles) 2.20 Three-Axle Commercial Vehicles 2.40 Heavy Construction Machinery (HCM) or Earth Moving Equipment (EME) or Multi Axle Vehicle. (MAV) (four to six axles) 3.45 Oversized Vehicles. (seven or more axles) 4.20 1. The fee levied and collected hereunder shall be due and payable at the following Toll Plazas for the distance specified for such Toll Plazas: Location of Toll Plazas (chainage) Length (in Km) for which Fee is payable Rate at which the fee is payable Existing Chainage km 216.600 (Design Chainage 216.545) on NH-7 (near village Joginhai in Rewa District) 34.17 100% of the base rates Existing Chainage km 147.600 (Design Chainage 148.100) on NH-7 (near village Masuriha in Rewa District) 27.16 100% of the base rates -- 4 of 6 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3(ii)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 5 2. In addition to the above, the following fee shall also be due and payable for the use of following bypass at the base rate mentioned in the Table below, cost of which is specified hereunder and at the following toll plaza: Location of Toll Plazas (chainage) Name & Length (in Km) of the bypass for which additional Fee is payable Cost of Bypass/ realignment (Rs. in crore) Rate at which the fee is payable Existing Chainage km 216.600 (Design Chainage 216.545) on NH-7 (near village Joginhai in Rewa District) Raipur Bypass of 4.16 km length taking off at km 229.600 [Design Chainage 222.600] and merging at km 218.700 [Design Chainage 218.440] 16.99 150% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above Mangawan Bypass of 4.20 km length taking off at km 208.300 [Design Chainage 207.960] and merging at km 204.100 [Design Chainage 203.760] 17.15 150% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above Ragunathganj Bypass of 3.68 km length taking off at km 197.400 [Design Chainage 197.100] and merging at km 193.700 [Design Chainage 193.420] 15.03 150% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above Lour and Devtalab Bypass of 3.42 km length taking off at km 189.600 [Design Chainage 189.360] and merging at km 186.200 [Design Chainage 185.940] 13.97 150% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above Existing Chainage km 147.600 (Design Chainage 148.100) on NH-7 (near village Masuriha in Rewa District) Mauganj Bypass of 6.60 km length taking off at km 174.700 [Design Chainage 174.300] and merging at km 168.100 [Design Chainage 167.700] 26.96 150% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above Hanumana Bypass of 5.84 km length taking off at km 147.900 [Design Chainage 147.540] and merging at km 142.100 [Design Chainage 141.700] 23.85 150% of the base rates mentioned in Table 1 above 3. The rate of monthly pass for a person who owns a mechanical vehicle registered for non-commercial purposes and resides within a distance of twenty kilometers from the toll plaza is Rs 225.00 (Rupees Two Hundred Twenty Five only) for the year 2014-15 and is subject to revision every year as per the provisions of the National Highway fee (determination of Rates and collection) Rules, 2008. 4. For multiple journeys on the highway section, passes shall be issued at the following rates namely: Amount Payable Maximum number of one way journeys allowed Period of validity One and half times of the fee for one way journey Two Twenty four hours from the time of payment Two – third of amount of the fee payable for fifty or more single journeys Fifty or more One month from date of payment 5. Fee for commercial vehicles (excluding vehicles plying under National Permit) registered in the district where the fee plaza falls shall be 50% of the prescribed rate for that category of vehicle provided no service road or alternative road is available for use of such commercial vehicles. 6. The driver or owner or a person in charge of a Mechanical vehicle which is loaded in excess of permissible load specified for its category, shall be liable to pay fee for entering the overloaded vehicle on the National Highway to the toll collecting agency, equal to ten times of the fee applicable to such category of mechanical vehicle and shall not be entitled to make use of the highway section unless the excess load has been removed from such mechanical vehicle. 7. All definitions, category of mechanical vehicles, its permissible load, category of exempted vehicles, service road, alternative road etc. shall be as per definitions specified in the said Rule. -- 5 of 6 -- 6 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(ii)] 8. The fee rates shall be displayed for information of the users in accordance with rules 12 of the said Rules. 9. Based on the base rate of fee per km for the base year 2007-08 as mentioned in Table-1, the actual amount of fee to be charged from the mechanical vehicles and the discounts will be calculated by the Authority on the basis of the completed portion of the length of the project Highway. The same shall be got published by the Authority in at least one newspaper, each in English and vernacular language through the concessionaire and thereafter revised annually in accordance with the rule 5 of the said rules and provision of Concession Agreement and got published, provided that no revision shall be effected with in a period of six months from the date of the preceding revision of fee.
